tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to do their part because they have plans, which feels nice but makes me worry about finishing my own share later. I'll probably just say yes now so we don't have a awkward conversation.

desires: Make sure Teammate A doesn't feel bad or awkward about asking me to cover their work right now.
Finish this extra task quickly so I can get back to hanging out with the cohort later tonight.
Avoid having a complicated conversation about why I'm doing someone else's job instead of my own.

goals.description: Reply to teammate_A with a soft, non-confrontational acceptance of their request to cover the work, emphasizing that I'm happy to help since they are busy.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is dominant; letting them down or making them feel awkward would cause immediate social friction. Avoiding conflict and maintaining harmony outweighs the fact that this isn't my original task.

goals.description: Start the extra task immediately in short bursts so I can finish it before we hang out with the cohort tonight, avoiding any need to explain why I'm late or distracted.
goals.rationale: I have a short planning horizon and high risk tolerance for procrastination, but the desire to see the friends later tonight acts as a strong immediate motivator. I'll just knock it out quickly rather than planning a deep dive.

agent.last_reasoning: I'll knock out their part quickly so we can grab dinner with the cohort tonight without awkward explanations. I'll text them later to say thanks, but finishing now keeps the peace and avoids stress.
